hero.title: Secure your code.
hero.subtitle: Scan your Git repositories with 7 industry-standard analyzers. Map results to the OWASP Top 10. Fix and open a Pull Request.

features.card2Desc: of vulnerabilities detected before production deployment.

howItWorks.title: How it
howItWorks.subtitle: From Git repository to security report, in 6 automated steps.

steps.title: Git Repository
steps.desc: Enter a Git repository URL. The platform clones and prepares the project for analysis.

steps.title: Parallel Analysis
steps.desc: 7 analyzers run in parallel: Semgrep, ESLint, npm audit, pip audit, Composer audit, TruffleHog and Bandit.

steps.title: OWASP Mapping
steps.desc: Each vulnerability is automatically categorized against the OWASP Top 10 2025 framework.

steps.title: Dashboard
steps.desc: Global score, severity charts, OWASP radar, file-level details. Everything is visual.

steps.title: Auto-Fix
steps.desc: 15+ fix patterns applied automatically. AI suggests corrections for the rest.

steps.title: Automatic PR
steps.desc: Fix branch created, corrections committed, Pull Request opened automatically.

items.a: SecureScan orchestrates 7 open-source analyzers: Semgrep (multi-language SAST), ESLint Security (JavaScript/Node.js), npm audit (JS dependencies), pip audit (Python dependencies), Composer audit (PHP dependencies), TruffleHog (secret detection in Git history), and Bandit (Python security). Each tool runs in parallel via Celery for fast scanning.

items.q: Do I need to install anything?
items.a: No. SecureScan runs in Docker — all analyzers are pre-installed in the backend container. Just run docker compose up and the application is accessible at localhost. To scan a repo, simply enter the Git URL.

The mapping algorithm follows 6 steps: check Semgrep OWASP metadata, default mapping by tool, Bandit ID matching, ESLint rule matching, keyword matching on rule_id and message, and fallback if no match. Every category A01 to A10 is covered.

15+ patterns cover the most common vulnerabilities: SQL injection (prepared statements), XSS (DOMPurify/textContent), eval/exec (safe alternatives), command injection, hardcoded secrets, MD5/SHA1, debug mode, CORS wildcard, cookies without flags, insecure deserialization, and more.

For vulnerabilities not covered by patterns, SecureScan sends the vulnerable code and its context to the Claude API. The LLM returns a drop-in fix with an explanation and confidence score. Only corrections with confidence >= 70% are applied automatically.
Yes. After corrections, SecureScan creates a fix/securescan-<id> branch, commits all modifications with a detailed message, pushes to the remote and opens a Pull Request via the GitHub API.

cta.title: Ready to secure
cta.subtitle: Start your first scan in under a minute. No installation required — just a Git repository URL.

footer.description: Security analysis and code quality platform. Open-source, DevSecOps-ready.

owasp.title: OWASP Top 10
owasp.subtitle: Every detected vulnerability is mapped to these 10 categories. Full coverage guaranteed.

categories.name: Broken Access Control
categories.desc: IDOR, privilege escalation, SSRF, URL manipulation.

categories.name: Security Misconfiguration
categories.desc: Missing headers, default configs, exposed stack traces, XXE.

categories.name: Supply Chain Failures
categories.desc: Compromised dependencies, malicious packages, insecure CI/CD.

categories.name: Cryptographic Failures
categories.desc: Plaintext data, MD5/SHA1, expired certificates, unencrypted transmission.

categories.name: Injection
categories.desc: SQL injection, XSS, command injection, template injection.

categories.name: Insecure Design
categories.desc: No rate limiting, flawed business logic, mass assignment.

categories.name: Authentication Failures
categories.desc: Brute force, poor session management, missing MFA, weak passwords.

categories.name: Integrity Failures
categories.desc: Insecure deserialization, insecure CI/CD, updates without verification.

categories.name: Logging & Alerting Failures
categories.desc: No security logs, no alerts, no traceability.

categories.name: Exceptional Conditions
categories.desc: Poor error handling, fail open, info leakage via error messages.
